what_it_does: Defines a dependency-ordered completion checklist (truth-table) for a registry-generation chain that reads mockups, proposes address-registry entries for UI elements, deduplicates and confirms them, and writes them back only after operator approval.
observations: Lists 11 criteria RG1–RG11 in dependency order from EXTRACT to GENERALIZES.
Every criterion is two-faced: FUNCTION (verified-by-use behavior) and FORM (operator-facing quality).
Status marks use ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 built-untested, 🔴 not-built/needs-tim; every item is currently 🔴.
Cross-lane tags [M], [C], [J] assign ownership to guided-review, cognition, or joint work.
Laws include registry-is-truth, no-hardcoding, operator-only floor, no-fiction, verify-by-USE, reuse-not-parallel.
Reuses existing tools: parse.py, screen_reader, run_reduce, run_jury, refcheck, cascade runner.
RG8 mandates a batch-review surface with simple-consent approval, not auto-write.
RG10 is the end-to-end gate: a previously unregistered element must resolve richly after the chain.
RG11 scopes the same chain to live app surfaces but is explicitly forward work.
standouts: Operator-only floor: PROPOSE never auto-write; Tim must approve a batch before write-back.
Whole-by-use gate: the chain is not done until a dead element becomes a richly resolving registry entry.
All statuses are 🔴, making the document an aspirational plan rather than a report of built state.
Explicit attention to the feel of the batch-review surface and chrome-level verification.
conventions: Consistent two-part heading per criterion: FUNCTION then FORM.
Dependency ordering and cross-lane ownership tags.
Reuse mandate instead of building parallel parsers/reducers.
concerns: Every criterion is marked not-built/needs-tim, so the document asserts criteria that cannot yet be verified.
Relies heavily on unspecified companion files and external components (screen_reader, run_reduce, etc.).
Some completion criteria include subjective design/feel language ('the feel of reviewing a batch').
RG11 is labeled forward scope but still appears in the priority-ordered list.
notes: This is the unimplemented, operator-gated completion checklist for a mockup-to-registry pipeline, currently all red and waiting on Tim approval.
questions: What exactly do the companion files RESEARCH-SYNTHESIS.md and IMPLEMENTATION-GUIDE.md contain?
What are V-A, V-B, and V-D and how do they relate to this chain?
Who is Tim and what does the simple-consent approval process look like?
Where are the 23 mockups located and what naming convention do they follow?
What is the concrete format of a run:// address and how are cascade outputs persisted?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a completion-criteria truth-table; the file matches that purpose but is entirely aspirational because every item is marked not-built.
apparent_themes: Registry as the single source of truth
Reuse existing substrate instead of parallel implementations
intention_signals: It is trying to define a rigorous, human-approved, reusable pipeline that discovers UI elements in mockups, registers them accurately, and grows the canonical address registry without hardcoding or hallucination.
novelty: The FUNCTION/FORM two-faced structure for each criterion and the explicit design-feel requirements for the operator batch-review surface are unusual for a completion-criteria document.
contribution: A structured, dependency-ordered checklist that names each pipeline stage, its success condition, ownership lane, reuse source, verification method, and governing laws, keeping the chain constrained to operator approval and grounded evidence.
summary_for_embedding: A completion-criteria truth-table for an operator-gated registry-generation chain that extracts UI elements from mockups, registers and deduplicates them, confirms accuracy, and writes approved entries into a canonical address registry.
intention_for_embedding: To define the verifiable stages and governance gates needed before a mockup-driven registry-generation pipeline can be considered complete.
